fundamentals.name: Starbucks Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Starbucks Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, operates as a roaster, marketer, and retailer of coffee internationally. The company operates through three segments: North America, International, and Channel Development. Its stores offer coffee, tea, and other beverages, roasted whole beans and ground coffees, complementary food, packaged coffees, single-serve products, and ready-to-drink beverages; and various food products, such as pastries, breakfast sandwiches, and lunch items. The company also licenses its trademarks through licensed stores, and grocery and foodservice accounts. The company offers its products under the Starbucks Coffee, Teavana, Seattle's Best Coffee, Ethos, and Starbucks Reserve brands. Starbucks Corporation was founded in 1971 and is based in Seattle, Washington.

judgment.summary: Starbucks shows strong earnings growth but faces headwinds from slowing revenue, high valuation, and negative free cash flow.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 14.5%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Earnings growth 33.0% YoY, beating analyst estimates for the current quarter.
Forward P/E of 33.74 is lower than trailing P/E of 77.96, suggesting expected earnings improvement.
Mean analyst target of $106.58 implies upside from the current price.
Dividend yield of 2.5% offers some return to shareholders.
negatives: Revenue growth of 8.8% YoY is modest, and analyst estimates for the full year are only 12.2%.
Trailing P/E of 77.96 is very high, indicating a rich valuation.
Free cash flow is negative at $-1.30B TTM, raising concerns about sustainability.
Dividend payout ratio of 187.8% is unsustainable.
Current ratio of 0.92 and quick ratio of 0.26 suggest potential short-term liquidity issues.

news.paragraph: Starbucks is ending its AI inventory program across North American stores due to inaccuracies, a move that follows a blunt message from the CEO regarding consumer spending and a recent analyst note highlighting slowing growth. While one report frames the company as a 'Bull of the Day,' the pullback on AI and broader consumer spending concerns suggest a mixed outlook. The company's next earnings date is July 28th.

headlines.title: Reuters: Starbucks Ends AI Inventory Program across North American Stores
